 {"id":"bd-kwjh","title":"Wisp storage: ephemeral molecule tracking","description":"Implement ephemeral molecule storage for patrol cycles.

## Architecture

Wisps are ephemeral molecules stored in `.beads-wisps/` (gitignored).
When squashed, they create digests in permanent `.beads/`.

**Storage is per-rig, not per-role**: Witness and Refinery share mayor/rig's 
`.beads-wisps/` since they execute from that context.

## Design Doc
See: gastown/mayor/rig/docs/wisp-architecture.md

## Key Requirements

1. **Ephemeral storage**: `.beads-wisps/` directory, gitignored
2. **Bond with --wisp**: Creates in wisps instead of permanent
3. **Squash**: Deletes wisp, creates digest in permanent beads
4. **Burn**: Deletes wisp, no digest
5. **Wisp commands**: `bd wisp list`, `bd wisp gc`

## Storage Locations

| Context | Location |
|---------|----------|
| Rig (Deacon, Witness, Refinery) | mayor/rig/.beads-wisps/ |
| Polecat (if used) | polecats/<name>/.beads-wisps/ |

## Children (to be created)
- bd mol bond --wisp flag
- .beads-wisps/ storage backend
- bd mol squash handles wisp to permanent
- bd wisp list command
- bd wisp gc command (orphan cleanup)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"beads/dave","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:34:47.188806-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:05:13.932186-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-kwjh.1","title":".beads-ephemeral/ storage backend","description":"Implement ephemeral storage layer for wisps.

## Requirements
- New storage location: .beads-ephemeral/issues.jsonl (sibling to .beads/)
- Gitignored by default (add to .beads/.gitignore)
- Same JSONL format as regular beads
- Config option: ephemeral.directory (relative path)
- ephemeral.enabled config flag

## Storage Behavior
- Ephemeral issues have `ephemeral: true` field
- No sync to remote (local only)
- No daemon tracking needed (transient)

## Implementation
- Add EphemeralStore in storage package
- Initialize on demand when --ephemeral flag used
- Share Issue struct, just different storage path","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:06:46.706026-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:08:26.009875-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.1","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:06:46.706461-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-22T00:08:26.009875-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"bd-kwjh.4","title":"bd mol squash handles wisp→digest","description":"Update bd mol squash to handle ephemeral molecules.

## Behavior for Ephemeral Molecules
1. Delete wisp from .beads-ephemeral/
2. Create digest issue in .beads/ (permanent)
3. Digest has type:digest and squashed_from field

## Digest Format
```json
{
  "id": "<parent>.digest-NNN",
  "type": "digest",
  "title": "<proto> cycle @ <timestamp>",
  "description": "<summary from --summary flag>",
  "parent": "<proto-id>",
  "squashed_from": "<wisp-id>"
}
```

## Implementation
- Detect if molecule is ephemeral (check storage location or flag)
- Delete from ephemeral store
- Create digest in permanent store
- Return digest ID

## Testing
- Test squash of ephemeral mol creates digest
- Test wisp is deleted after squash
- Test digest is queryable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:27.685116-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:27.685116-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:27.686798-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:27.687773-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kwjh.5","title":"bd wisp list command","description":"Add bd wisp list command to show ephemeral molecules.

## Usage
```bash
bd wisp list              # List all wisps in current context
bd wisp list --json       # JSON output
bd wisp list --all        # Include orphaned wisps
```

## Output
- Shows in-progress ephemeral molecules
- Columns: ID, Title, Started, Last Update, Status
- Warns about orphaned wisps (old updated_at)

## Implementation
- New 'wisp' command group
- Read from .beads-ephemeral/issues.jsonl
- Filter to ephemeral:true issues","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:29.514936-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:29.514936-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.5","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:29.515301-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.5","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:29.516134-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kwjh.6","title":"bd wisp gc command","description":"Add bd wisp gc command to garbage collect orphaned wisps.

## Usage
```bash
bd wisp gc                # Clean orphaned wisps
bd wisp gc --dry-run      # Show what would be cleaned
bd wisp gc --age 1h       # Custom orphan threshold (default: 1h)
```

## Orphan Detection
A wisp is orphaned if:
- process_id field exists AND process is dead
- OR updated_at older than threshold AND not complete
- AND molecule status is not complete/abandoned

## Behavior
- Delete orphaned wisps (no digest created)
- Report count of cleaned wisps
- --dry-run shows candidates without deleting

## Implementation
- Add 'gc' subcommand to wisp group
- Process detection via os.FindProcess or /proc
- Configurable age threshold","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:30.861155-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:30.861155-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:30.862681-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-kwjh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-kwjh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T00:07:30.863721-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
